title: Ecbolium fragile
taxonomy: Ecbolium fragile is classified in the family Acanthaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or thyme family. It belongs to the order Lamiales, which includes plants with bilaterally symmetrical flowers and four- or five-merous calyces. The genus Ecbolium is a part of the subfamily Thunbergioideae, characterized by its unique flowers and distinctive leaf structures. Ecbolium fragile specifically falls under the section Thunbergia, which includes plants with similar characteristics and growth habits. This classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships and diversification of Ecbolium fragile and its close relatives.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are known to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
